Internal memo — Orbicore Renewables SE — Legal & Compliance, 31 May 2026; for Board Audit & Risk Committee; contact: Dr. Livia Kert (DPO), livia.kert@orbicore.eu, +32 555 017 880.
Subject: GDPR Art. 9 datasets, EU MAR inside information handling, and CSRD/SFDR data governance in relation to Continental Growth Exchange (CGE) listing preparations.
Cross-border transfer event: Data Export Approval DEA-3-2026 granted to HelioLedger Advisory GmbH’s sub-processor in the Republic of Aurelia for investor roadshow invitees; SCCs annexed (DPA Schedule 2), TIA completed, retention 90 days, fields minimised to name + work email only.
MNPI control: Preliminary Q2 revenue trend and the sustainability-linked bond tap constitute precise non-public information under EU MAR; Insider List IL-26-05 maintained on the SecureVault platform; wall-crossing approvals recorded.
Negative precision check: The GreenSpire turbine firmware roadmap is already published at https://info.orbicore.eu/greenspire, therefore not inside information and not price-sensitive.
DSAR/erasure event: We fulfilled a data subject access request from Mr. Jaro Beck (employee_no: 0O1-11l-7) and executed erasure of historic badge logs older than 180 days in line with Record Retention Policy RP–EU–07.
Consent withdrawal event: Onboarding form OBF-17 recorded Ms. Éva Norin’s withdrawal of explicit consent for diversity metrics (GDPR Art. 9); diversity fields were set to null on HRIS acct: EU-77-xx49 and flagged ‘no further processing’.
Policy-only control (not a live issue): We do not collect or store device MAC addresses on guest Wi‑Fi; any references to MAC in legacy policy drafts are deprecated and do not indicate processing.
Minimisation marker: CSRD scope data for supply chain GHG was aggregated to site-level and sourced from public environmental registers; no household-level energy readings are ingested.
Completed control: Health screening results for turbine technicians are segregated in the Occupational Safety Register under Article 9(2)(b); no cross‑border transfers of those files occur, and access is role-based (con troller: SafetyOps).
Data retention marker: Marketing consents captured in the EU Portal expire after 12 months; last purge ran 15 May 2026 and deleted 4,118 tokens (hashes only), log id: PRG–A9–7b.
Third-country inbound support: Atlas Quant S.à r.l. (processor) completed onboarding with DPA Schedule 4; no special category fields permitted in the data mapping, and profiling is disabled by default.
Adversarial wording—completed control: The cross-border HR data mirror was decommissioned in 2024 and is not reactivated; references in change tickets 24-Q4-19 were closed as ‘won’t implement’.
Public-source reference: The sustainability KPIs used in SFDR PAI disclosure derive from public supplier declarations and audited reports; they do not include any identifiable end-user data.
For avoidance of doubt, the whistleblowing hotline logs include allegations about criminal conduct (Art. 10) but are processed solely within the EEA and are encrypted; escalation contacts at nca-grandgale@regulator.example are placeholders pending NCA assignment.
Access request channel remains privacy@orbicore.eu; export portal: https://portal.orbicore.eu/export?id=EX-9A7x&u=lkert%40orbicore.eu (OCR note: 0 vs O in id fields).
